rulings.Case Title: LVNV FUNDING LLC VS. JUAN S RUIZ ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion Calendar for Thursday, October 17, 2024, Line 1, PLAINTIFF LVNV FUNDING LLC's Motion To Enforce Settlement (C.C.P. Section 664.6). Plaintiff's unopposed "motion to enforce settlement" is granted. For the 9:30 a.m. Law & Motion calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for plaintiff is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(302/RBU)

rulings.Case Title: FIRST NATIONAL BANK OF OMAHA VS. JORGE A MARTINEZ ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Hearing On Claim Of Exemption
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion Calendar for Thursday, October 17, 2024, Line 3, PLAINTIFF FIRST NATIONAL BANK OF OMAHA, A NATIONAL BANKING ASSOCIATION's Hearing On Claim Of Exemption. Defendant Jorge Martinez's claim of exemption is denied. The levying officer is directed to withhold $100 per pay period (every two weeks) from defendant's earnings. For the 9:30 a.m. Law & Motion calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. If the court adopts the tentative ruling, it will execute a judicial council form of order reflecting the ruling. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. rulings.Case Title: CENTER FOR ADVANCED PUBLIC AWARENESS VS. LEICA MICROSYSTEMS INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of And Motion To Continue Hearing On Defendants Motion For Summary Judgment And To Stay Discovery
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion Calendar for Thursday, October 17, 2024, Line 6, PLAINTIFF CENTER FOR ADVANCED PUBLIC AWARENESS's Motion To Continue Hearing On Defendants Motion For Summary Judgment And To Stay Discovery. The court exercises its discretion to continue the pending summary judgment hearing to January 28, 2025. Plaintiff says its CPA failed to file its taxes and it is seeking a certificate of revivor from state officials. (See Color Vue, Inc. v. Abrams (1996) 1599, 1606.) Motions for trial continuance and related motions are exclusively handled by Department 206 For the 9:30 a.m. Law & Motion calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for plaintiff is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. rulings.Case Title: FUNG LEE VS. THE REGENTS OF THE UNIVERSITY OF CALIFORNIA ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings Directed At Defendants Affirmative Defenses [Non-Statutory Jop Motion]
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion Calendar for Thursday, October 17, 2024, Line 7, PLAINTIFFS FUNG LEE AS PERSONAL REPRESENTATIVE OF THE ESTATE OF GIM LEE, AND ALBERT LEE's Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings Directed At Defendants Affirmative Defenses [Non-Statutory JOP Motion]. Plaintiff's "motion for judgment on the pleadings directed at defendants' affirmative defenses" is denied. The affirmative defenses are in answers filed back in September 2023. (See Opp. 1:19-25.) Trial is now less than a month away. As a leading treatise states: "Trial judges are often reluctant to grant last minute challenges to the pleadings because they disrupt the court's trial calendar and may result in unnecessary trial preparation expenses for both sides." (Edmon & Karnow, California Practice Guide: Civil Procedure Before Trial (TRG 2016) 7:287.) For the 9:30 a.m. Law & Motion calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for plaintiff is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(302/RBU)

rulings.Case Title: MATTHEW HEIDEMAN ET AL VS. D2IQ, INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Amended Notice Of Demurrer And Demurrer To Plaintiffs' Amended Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion Calendar for Thursday, October 17, 2024, Line 8, DEFENDANTS BRUCE ARMSTRONG, BRENDON DURKIN, PETER LEVINE, ANN LIVERMORE, and JOSEPH SEXTON's Demurrer To Plaintiffs' Amended Complaint. Ordered off calendar for plaintiffs' noncompliance with California Rule of Court 3.1113: (d) (15-page limit) and (f) (tables of contents and authorities). Demurrer may be re-set for hearing on a Mon.-Thur. after October 31. Plaintiffs' papers shall bear new hearing date. Defendants complied with the rules of court and thus need not re-file their papers. For the 9:30 a.m. Law & Motion calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely. rulings.Case Title: NEW JAMESTOWN LP VS. CANNON CONSTRUCTORS, INC., ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS OR STAY OR DISMISS
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion Calendar for Thursday, October 17, 2024, Line 11, DEFENDANT UPONOR NORTH AMERICA, INC.S MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS AND FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT FOR LACK OF PERSONAL JURISDICTION. Plaintiff's request for jurisdictional discovery is granted. (See Mihlon v. Sup. Ct. (1985) 169 Cal.App.3d 703, 710.) That discovery period shall end December 17, followed by a case management conference at 9:30 a.m. on December 23. The remainder of the case is stayed until December 23. rulings.Case Title: NAUMAN NADEEM VS. PARADIGM HOTELS GROUP LLC, A CALIFORNIA LIMITED ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion Calendar for Thursday, October 17, 2024, Line 14, DEFENDANTS PARADIGM HOTELS GROUP, LLC, AND 250 FOURTH DEVELOPMENT, LP'S MOTION TO COMPEL ARBITRATION. Defendant's motion to compel arbitration and stay is denied. Defendant failed to timely pay all of the required arbitration costs. (Sarris Decl., pars. 10-11, 15.) Therefore, per CCP 1281.98, plaintiff can litigate this case in court. Plaintiff may seek fees/sanctions pursuant to CCP 1281.98(c) and CCP 1281.99 by a separate regularly noticed motion. For the 9:30 a.m. Law & Motion calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(302/RBU)
